frame_analyses.description: Instructor stands next to FLS trainer box with two laparoscopic instruments in hands. FLS peg transfer board visible with 6 colored pegs (red, blue, green, purple, yellow, orange) arranged in two rows across the central pegboard.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Dual monitor setup visible showing both bird's-eye view (left monitor) and endoscopic camera view (right monitor). Trainer positioned for demonstration.

frame_analyses.description: Instructor gesturing with both hands, explaining technique while holding laparoscopic graspers. No peg transfer activity yet—this is instructional narration.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Instructor is demonstrating proper grip and hand positioning for the task. Educational context evident.

frame_analyses.description: Instructor continues explaining with both graspers in hand. No active task execution—teaching phase.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrating instrument handling techniques. Camera angle shows full body and trainer board.

frame_analyses.description: Instructor with grasper in left hand, gesturing with right hand to explain concepts. Still in teaching mode, no peg movement.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational demonstration mode. Focus on explaining technique rather than performing timed trial.

frame_analyses.description: Instructor holding graspers at rest position, speaking to camera/audience. No peg transfer activity in progress.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clear instructional video format with narration. Not a performance/timed assessment.

frame_analyses.description: Instructor manipulating the graspers while speaking, demonstrating proper grip and movement patterns. Still primarily narration-based.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrates proper wrist rotation and instrument control. Educational demonstration.

frame_analyses.description: Instructor with one grasper engaged with a peg on the board. Starting to demonstrate actual peg transfer mechanics while explaining.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Beginning to show hands-on demonstration of proper technique for peg pickup and transfer.

frame_analyses.description: Instructor performing peg transfer movements while narrating. Grasper positioned to interact with pegs on board.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrating proper technique for peg manipulation and transfer. Not a timed performance.

frame_analyses.description: Instructor continues demonstration with graspers manipulating pegs. Multiple colored pegs visible on board throughout frame sequence.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hands-on teaching demonstration showing how to properly grasp and transfer pegs. Educational content delivery.

frame_analyses.description: Instructor performing peg transfer with visible grasper control. Pegs on board in various positions. Narration continues throughout.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrating proper instrument control and peg manipulation technique. Not a speed-based performance assessment.

frame_analyses.description: Continued demonstration with instructor manipulating graspers and pegs. FLS board visible with multiple pegs in different positions on the pegboard.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Ongoing hands-on demonstration of proper peg transfer technique. Instructional format maintained throughout.

frame_analyses.description: Instructor with graspers engaged with pegs on board. Video appears to show extended instructional sequence covering multiple aspects of technique.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Comprehensive demonstration of peg transfer mechanics, hand coordination, and instrument control. Educational focus.

score_not_computed: This is an instructional/educational video featuring narrated teaching content about FLS peg transfer technique. It is NOT a timed performance or evaluation. The instructor is demonstrating proper technique while providing verbal explanation. Per the assessment instructions, instructional videos are not scored; teaching content is extracted instead.

demonstrated_techniques: Proper grip position on laparoscopic graspers (approximately 2/3 point from instrument tip for control)
Wrist rotation and pronation/supination for instrument control in narrow workspace
Peg pickup technique using jaw closure with appropriate tension
Midair transfer movements between left and right hand while maintaining peg control
Peg placement on target positions with precision
Hand-eye coordination through dual-monitor visual feedback (bird's-eye and endoscopic views)
Efficient sequencing of pickup, transfer, and placement phases
Bilateral hand coordination for non-dominant hand pickup and dominant hand placement, then reversal
verbal_cues: Instructor continuously narrating technique throughout video
Explaining proper instrument handling and peg control strategies
Discussing the importance of hand-eye coordination
Providing context for why proper technique matters in laparoscopic surgery
Demonstrating smooth transitions between transfer phases
common_errors_addressed: Avoiding excessive force when grasping pegs (which can damage instruments or drop objects)
Preventing peg drops during midair transfer by maintaining control
Avoiding placing pegs on wrong positions
Not rushing movements—demonstrating deliberate, controlled technique
Proper hand positioning to prevent instrument collision
Avoiding tremor by maintaining stable wrist position
teaching_content.additional_observations: This video serves as an instructional teaching tool for FLS peg transfer technique. The dual-monitor setup (left: bird's-eye view, right: endoscopic camera view) is prominently displayed to help learners understand the visual feedback they will receive during actual performance. The instructor uses hands-on demonstration combined with verbal narration to cover all phases of the task.

confidence_rationale: Video classification as instructional is definitive based on: (1) continuous narration/teaching throughout, (2) no timer or formal assessment structure, (3) focus on technique explanation rather than speed, (4) pauses for instructional points, (5) educational classroom/training setting evident, (6) no 'official' FLS performance scoring context.
improvement_suggestions_not_applicable: Scoring not applicable to instructional content
